title: Apparitions - Prologue To "The Two Poets Of Croisic."
content:
Such a starved bank of moss
    Till, that May-morn,
    Blue ran the flash across:
    Violets were born!

    Sky, what a scowl of cloud
    Till, near and far,
    Ray on ray split the shroud:
    Splendid, a star!

    World, how it walled about
    Life with disgrace,
    Till God's own smile came out:
    That was thy face!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Browning, titled "Apparitions - Prologue To "The Two Poets Of Croisic."", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
